title: quercus ilex subvar. reducta
intro: Quercus ilex subvar. reducta, also known as the cork oak, is a subspecies of the holm oak tree, characterized by its smaller size and more compact growth habit compared to other Quercus ilex varieties. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus ilex subvar. reducta include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as showing potential in fighting certain types of cancer. Quercus ilex subvar. reducta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
